results.name: CoachingSessionDetail page loads
results.reasoning: The page shows a loading spinner in the center of the main content area, which is the expected behavior when trying to load a session with a fake/non-existent ID. The app shell (sidebar navigation, header) loaded correctly without crashing, and the loading state indicates the feature is working as intended - React Query is attempting to fetch the session data.

results.name: TrainingPlans page loads
results.reasoning: The Training Plans page is displaying correctly with the expected empty state. Shows the dumbbell icon, '0 plans' count, and 'No training plans yet' message, which is appropriate for a test account without organization membership or existing plans.

results.name: TrainingPlanDetail page loads
results.reasoning: Shows correct 'Plan not found' error message with 'Back to Plans' button - this is the expected behavior for a nonexistent plan ID and demonstrates proper error handling

results.name: MyTraining page loads
results.reasoning: The page displays a proper 'My Training' section with dumbbell icon, correct subtitle 'Your personalized training plan from your coach', and shows an appropriate empty state message 'No training plan assigned yet' with explanatory text 'Your coach will create one for you soon'. This is expected behavior for test accounts without assigned training plans.

results.name: Return to Feed from coaching features
results.reasoning: Feed page is functioning correctly with real content - user stories at top show actual profiles (Marcus, Zara, Sofia, Diego, Jack), post creation input is active, sports category filters are available (For You, Following, All, Basketball, Soccer, Football), and there's a visible post from Marcus Silva with soccer content including an image and engagement options. The navigation shows Feed is currently selected and all UI elements appear functional.
